이사야 33:1.content: Woe to you who destroy, but you weren’t destroyed, and who betray, but nobody betrayed you! When you have finished destroying, you will be destroyed; and when you have finished betrayal, you will be betrayed.

이사야 33:2.content: Yahweh, be gracious to us. We have waited for you. Be our strength every morning, our salvation also in the time of trouble.

이사야 33:3.content: At the noise of the thunder, the peoples have fled. When you lift yourself up, the nations are scattered.

이사야 33:4.content: Your plunder will be gathered as the caterpillar gathers. Men will leap on it as locusts leap.

이사야 33:5.content: Yahweh is exalted, for he dwells on high. He has filled Zion with justice and righteousness.

이사야 33:6.content: There will be stability in your times, abundance of salvation, wisdom, and knowledge. The fear of Yahweh is your treasure.

이사야 33:7.content: Behold, their valiant ones cry outside; the ambassadors of peace weep bitterly.

이사야 33:8.content: The highways are desolate. The traveling man ceases. The covenant is broken. He has despised the cities. He doesn’t respect man.

이사야 33:9.content: The land mourns and languishes. Lebanon is confounded and withers away. Sharon is like a desert, and Bashan and Carmel are stripped bare.

이사야 33:10.content: “Now I will arise,” says Yahweh. “Now I will lift myself up. Now I will be exalted.

이사야 33:11.content: You will conceive chaff. You will give birth to stubble. Your breath is a fire that will devour you.

이사야 33:12.content: The peoples will be like the burning of lime, like thorns that are cut down and burned in the fire.

이사야 33:13.content: Hear, you who are far off, what I have done; and, you who are near, acknowledge my might.”

이사야 33:14.content: The sinners in Zion are afraid. Trembling has seized the godless ones. Who among us can live with the devouring fire? Who among us can live with everlasting burning?

이사야 33:15.content: He who walks righteously and speaks blamelessly, he who despises the gain of oppressions, who gestures with his hands, refusing to take a bribe, who stops his ears from hearing of blood, and shuts his eyes from looking at evil—

이사야 33:16.content: he will dwell on high. His place of defense will be the fortress of rocks. His bread will be supplied. His waters will be sure.

이사야 33:17.content: Your eyes will see the king in his beauty. They will see a distant land.

이사야 33:18.content: Your heart will meditate on the terror. Where is he who counted? Where is he who weighed? Where is he who counted the towers?

이사야 33:19.content: You will no longer see the fierce people, a people of a deep speech that you can’t comprehend, with a strange language that you can’t understand.

이사야 33:20.content: Look at Zion, the city of our appointed festivals. Your eyes will see Jerusalem, a quiet habitation, a tent that won’t be removed. Its stakes will never be plucked up, nor will any of its cords be broken.

이사야 33:21.content: But there Yahweh will be with us in majesty, a place of wide rivers and streams, in which no galley with oars will go, neither will any gallant ship pass by there.

이사야 33:22.content: For Yahweh is our judge. Yahweh is our lawgiver. Yahweh is our king. He will save us.

이사야 33:23.content: Your rigging is untied. They couldn’t strengthen the foot of their mast. They couldn’t spread the sail. Then the prey of a great plunder was divided. The lame took the prey.

이사야 33:24.content: The inhabitant won’t say, “I am sick.” The people who dwell therein will be forgiven their iniquity.
